title: STAT+: Democrats prepare for health care overhaul
summary: Democrats are outlining a new vision for the health care system ahead of the midterms.

title: FDA Approves Fourth Product Under National Priority Voucher Program, Higher Dose Semaglutide
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved a new higher dose (7.2 mg) of Wegovy (semaglutide) injection for weight loss and long-term maintenance of weight loss for certain adult patients.

title: STAT+: Novo Nordisk’s high-dose Wegovy approved in the U.S.
summary: A high-dose version of Novo Nordisk’s injectable obesity drug Wegovy received U.S. approval.

title: Lilly’s three-pronged obesity drug hits goal in large diabetes trial
summary: Retatrutide, a possible advance on GLP-1 drugs like Zepbound, significantly cut blood sugar and body weight in the study, a sign it might be similarly impactful against obesity.

title: Pfizer aims for wider use of PARP drug in prostate cancer with new data
summary: While sales of Talzenna, which Pfizer acquired in a $14 billion deal, have been “negligible” so far, the new results should justify use in earlier lines of care, one analyst said.

title: STAT+: Eli Lilly’s ‘triple-G’ shows potency, raises concerns
summary: Eli Lilly's 'triple-G' drug shows potency, In-vivo CAR-T inches toward clinical reality, and more in today's Readout newsletter.

title: STAT+: A biotech turns to Apple Watch to study its Parkinson’s drug
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: How Apple Watch is being used in a clinical trial for a Parkinson's drug, and more

title: A biotech VC sees early signs of a turnaround for startups
summary: After a lengthy downturn, renewed optimism is building among young drugmakers and their backers as a new funding wave takes shape, said Santé Ventures’ Omar Khalil.

title: Did we undercount Covid deaths by 155,000?
summary: In today's Morning Rounds newsletter: the precarious state of U.S. science, what we take for granted about vaccines, and more.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a Lilly weight loss trial, TrumpRx shortcomings, and more
summary: Diabetes patients taking an investigational drug from Eli Lilly saw significant reductions in their blood sugar and weight in a late-stage study

title: AstraZeneca to boost cell therapy capabilities with new China investment
summary: The planned Shanghai facility extends a yearslong push into the field and will support CAR-T therapies AstraZeneca is making for autoimmune disease and cancer.

title: STAT+: Eli Lilly’s ‘triple-G’ drug leads to significant blood sugar, weight reductions in diabetes trial
summary: Diabetes patients taking an investigational drug from Eli Lilly saw significant reductions in their blood sugar and weight in a late-stage study.

title: STAT+: Six drugmakers, six months: Charting the FDA’s course in the post-Prasad era
summary: Will the FDA’s regulatory policies, particularly for rare-disease drugs, shift in the wake of Vinay Prasad’s imminent departure?

title: STAT+: Senate Democrats lay out plans to overhaul health insurance after setbacks under Trump
summary: Democrats are sharpening their health care message as the midterms loom.

title: Researchers surveyed by STAT detail the toll of grant cuts: labs shutting down, data unanalyzed
summary: STAT interviews with 30 respondents brought the unmistakably human impacts of federal science policy into sharp focus. Here are three of those stories.

title: The indirect — and sometimes surprising — benefits of vaccines
summary: Many vaccines confer surprising knock-on, or indirect, health benefits when they have been put into widespread use.

title: National survey of NIH-funded researchers shows precarious state of U.S. science — ‘This is like the Titanic’
summary: A nationwide STAT survey of NIH-funded researchers reveals that, a year after Donald Trump's return to the White House, many scientists are reeling

title: Opinion: There is a successor to 340B lurking on the horizon
summary: The closer we get to 2028, the more likely it is that the U.S. may settle for short-term solutions that do not meaningfully reform the health care system.

title: STAT+: HHS watchdog slams nursing homes’ antipsychotic drug use and schizophrenia diagnoses
summary: Nursing homes misuse antipsychotic drugs for dementia patients, disguise practice as schizophrenia treatment, watchdog finds.

title: STAT+: FDA’s top infectious disease regulator to depart agency
summary: Adam Sherwat, the director of the Office of Infectious Diseases in the FDA’s Center for Drug Evaluation and Research, is leaving the agency.

title: More than 150,000 uncounted Covid-19 deaths occurred early in the pandemic, a study finds
summary: The Covid-19 pandemic's early death toll was much higher than the official U.S. count, according to a new study that spotlights dramatic disparities in the uncounted deaths.

title: STAT+: A Maryland legislator wants pharma and patient groups to disclose ties to disease awareness campaigns
summary: A bill introduced in the Maryland legislature would require drugmakers to disclose ties to disease awareness campaigns.

title: RBC: Revolution, Xenon, Arrowhead among top takeover targets
summary: As part of a larger review, analyst Leonid Timashev identified 13 companies that could see deal interest as well as more than half a dozen poised to be potential buyers.

title: J&J wins FDA nod for psoriasis pill that aims to compete with top-selling injections
summary: The clearance is the first for an oral peptide that blocks IL-23, the target of popular medicines like Skyrizi and Tremfya. J&J has said the new drug, Icotyde, has the potential to top $5 billion in…

title: STAT+: Clearing tumors in mice, Azalea Therapeutics advances dream of in vivo CAR-T therapy
summary: A mouse study shows CAR-T cells can be engineered in vivo with precise gene editing, a potential breakthrough that could cut costs and expand access.

title: Aspen’s fresh Parkinson’s data; Aldeyra tanks following FDA rejection
summary: Aspen Neuroscience presented positive Phase 1/2 data for its stem cell-derived therapy. Elsewhere, Aldeyra lost over half its market value following a rejection from the FDA.

title: STAT+: How ARPA-H is developing FDA-authorized AI agents, tested in clinical trials
summary: STAT's AI Prognosis takes a closer look at how the U.S. moonshot agency for health research is developing AI agents for deployment in hospitals.

title: STAT+: A Huntington’s researcher on the UniQure-FDA fray
summary: A Huntington's researcher treads lightly into the UniQure-FDA fray, J&J wins approval for a first-of-its kind psoriasis pill, and more in today's Readout newsletter.

title: Crossbow raises $77M for new cancer immunotherapies
summary: Backed by more than a dozen investors, the biotech startup will use the funding to further a Phase 1 trial of its lead program CBX-250.

title: FDA Releases Draft Guidance on Alternatives to Animal Testing in Drug Development
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued draft guidance intended to help drug developers validate new approach methodologies (NAMs) to be used instead of animal testing in drug development,…

title: Chinese biotech startup Excalipoint banks $69M for next-gen T cell engagers
summary: The funding, which ranks as one of the largest of its kind for a China-based drug startup, will support a portfolio of multifaceted antibodies the company believes to be advances over existing…

title: Non-incretin approach for weight loss
Bicycle to lay off 30% of staff, pivot away from Padcev challenger
summary: The company now expects a longer path to approval than anticipated, leading it to deprioritize a therapy billed as a threat to Pfizer’s fast-selling bladder cancer drug.

title: Upcoming FDA approval decisions in Q2 2026
